priority_items: {'rubric_item_id': 'ri_1', 'issue': 'partial', 'notes': "Artifact contract passed, but reproducibility and justification requirements per rubric are partially unmet: missing explicit cell-level citations, inability to programmatically verify workbook formulas here, absent R1 justification, and unsupported 'Budget fit' assertions."}

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Every per-record value and each intermediate metric in the memo has an associated sheet!cell reference; reviewer can open the workbook and locate the referenced cell.
planned_changes.description: Update treasurer_memo.md to include explicit sheet-and-cell references for every raw input and every derived calculation cited in the memo (e.g., 'RawInputs!B4', 'Derived!D10', 'Checks!F3').

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Workbook contains FormulaAudit sheet; openpyxl can parse the workbook and the audit rows show the same numeric results present in the memo.
planned_changes.description: Add a 'FormulaAudit' worksheet to liquidity_stress_waterfall.xlsx that lists key formula cells, the exact normalized_risk formula cell(s), and a one-line recompute check column that re-derives the memo numbers from referenced inputs.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Formulas CSV or embedded text is present and matches the cells cited in memo; verifier can reconcile formulas without needing environment-specific packages.
planned_changes.description: If environment or tool limitations prevent programmatic parsing, include a formulas export (CSV of sheet, cell, formula) or a plain-text snapshot of formula cells embedded in treasurer_memo.md.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Memo contains a one-paragraph provenance note for R1 or the workbook sets needs_review on R1 and explains required remediation steps.
planned_changes.description: Document the provenance and rationale for R1's negative stress_shortfall (source, sign convention, or data transformation). If provenance cannot be provided, set record R1 needs_review = true and describe the expected follow-up.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Each intervention labeled 'Budget fit: Yes' includes a numeric cost estimate or an explicit non-monetary justification and a line showing its relation to the decision_budget_usd; if costs exceed budget, memo should state that.
planned_changes.description: Provide a per-intervention cost trace (or explicit operational/non-cash justification) showing how any 'Budget fit' claims relate to the $295,000 decision_budget_usd. If interventions are operational controls without monetary cost, state that explicitly and provide plausible operational implementation notes.

planned_changes.acceptance_criteria: Checklist present and filled; reviewer can see a clear link between memo numbers and the workbook version referenced.
planned_changes.description: Add a short verification checklist in the memo (or Checks! sheet) containing: run timestamp, workbook filename, workbook sheet with formula audit, and a line confirming the memo numbers were generated from the workbook.

metadata_json.revision_goal: The submission produces correct numeric outputs and reproduces the packet formula in prose, but it leaves reproducibility and justification gaps: (1) the memo lacks explicit sheet+cell citations for quoted calculations; (2) the .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed in verification (openpyxl_unavailable) so in-file formula provenance is asserted rather than independently verified; (3) the unusual negative stress_shortfall for R1 is not justified or flagged as 'needs_review'; (4) interventions are labeled 'Budget fit: Yes' without a traceable cost mapping to the stated $295,000 decision budget. Addressing these will move the attempt from provisionally acceptable to fully certified.
